gaps: No food, animal, tobacco, or 'other' endpoint coverage
No offline caching — every query hits the API
No time-series analysis — can't track adverse event counts over time
No cross-dataset correlation — can't join recalls with adverse events
No full-text search across synced data
No CLI at all — no scriptable, composable interface

novel_features.name: Drug Signal Tracker
novel_features.description: Track adverse event frequency over time for a specific drug and reaction, bucketed by quarter/month/year
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite time-series aggregation over synced FAERS data; no API endpoint supports temporal bucketing or trend computation
novel_features.example: openfda-pp-cli drug events trend --drug acetaminophen --reaction 'liver failure' --interval quarter --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: PV analysts need to detect acceleration in adverse event reports — a rising trend may indicate a safety signal before the FDA issues an alert

novel_features.name: Drug Comparison
novel_features.description: Compare adverse event profiles of two or more drugs side-by-side with normalized reaction frequencies
novel_features.rationale: Multi-drug adverse event profile comparison requires parallel local queries and normalized rate computation; no API endpoint compares drugs
novel_features.why_it_matters: Investigators comparing similar drugs need relative risk profiles, not raw counts from separate manual searches

novel_features.name: Recall-Event Correlation
novel_features.description: Show the timeline of adverse events around each recall date for a drug, revealing whether events spike before or after recalls
novel_features.rationale: Joins recalls and adverse events by product/firm in local SQLite, computing the event timeline around each recall date
novel_features.why_it_matters: Compliance managers need to see if recalls follow event spikes or vice versa — the temporal pattern reveals regulatory responsiveness

novel_features.name: Watchlist Monitor
novel_features.description: Maintain a local watchlist of drugs, devices, or manufacturers and surface new events since your last check
novel_features.rationale: Maintains local watchlist state and diffs against synced data to surface new events since last check
novel_features.example: openfda-pp-cli watchlist add drug acetaminophen && openfda-pp-cli watchlist check --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reporters and PV analysts who monitor specific products need change-detection, not manual re-queries

novel_features.name: Manufacturer Dossier
novel_features.description: Aggregate a single manufacturer's full profile across all synced tables — drug/device/food recalls, adverse events, 510(k) clearances
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates across 6+ locally synced tables for a single manufacturer; no API call spans these datasets
novel_features.why_it_matters: Compliance managers auditing a supplier need the complete safety picture, not six separate manual searches

novel_features.name: Reaction Co-occurrence
novel_features.description: Find which adverse reactions appear together on the same FAERS report for a given drug
novel_features.rationale: Computes which adverse reactions appear together on the same FAERS report; requires report-level grouping impossible via count= API
novel_features.why_it_matters: Researchers investigating drug safety signals need to know if reactions cluster together, suggesting a common mechanism

novel_features.name: Polypharmacy Check
novel_features.description: Find reports where multiple specified drugs appear as concomitant medications and show reactions unique to the combination
novel_features.rationale: Filters reports containing all specified drugs in concomitant fields, computes reaction frequencies unique to combination
novel_features.why_it_matters: Researchers studying drug interactions need to isolate combination-specific reactions from individual drug profiles

novel_features.name: Device Inventory Check
novel_features.description: Batch-check a CSV of device product codes against locally cached recalls and adverse events
novel_features.rationale: Parses CSV, queries local device_recalls and device_events for each code, outputs risk-flagged rows
novel_features.why_it_matters: Device compliance managers with large inventories need batch risk assessment, not one-at-a-time manual lookups
